title: Actor Paapa Hopes TV Show Helps People Talk About Baby Loss
summary: Paapa Essiedu is an actor in a TV show called Babies. The show is about a couple named Lisa and Steven who are trying to have a baby but face a sad event called pregnancy loss. This means they lose the baby before it is born. Paapa wants the show to help people talk about this topic because many feel shy or alone. He worked with doctors and midwives to learn about it since he is not a parent. Another actor, Siobhán, also talked to women who had similar experiences. The show's writer, Stefan, made it honest and caring. The goal is to break the silence so people can share their feelings and not feel isolated.

when a baby is lost before it is born

background_read: Pregnancy loss is when a baby dies before it is born, which can make parents very sad. Many people do not talk about it because they feel embarrassed or alone. TV shows can help by showing stories that make it easier to discuss hard topics. Actors like Paapa Essiedu learn from experts to act realistically and spread awareness.
Article_Structure: WHO: Paapa Essiedu, an actor, and other cast members like Siobhán Cullen and writer Stefan Golaszewski.
WHAT: A TV drama called Babies about a couple facing pregnancy loss and trying to have a baby.
WHERE: In a TV show set in Britain, created for audiences to watch.
WHY: To help people talk about pregnancy loss and reduce feelings of shame and isolation.

perspectives.description: Paapa Essiedu hopes the show encourages open conversation about pregnancy loss.
